steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left side: independent clause (ending with “he authored many of his works”)
Right side: independent clause (beginning with “among these personas was Ricardo Reis...”)

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two related independent clauses. The correct answer uses a semicolon between them. The other three violate this rule:
  • One redundantly pairs a colon with the conjunction and
  • One uses and without proper parallel structure
  • One uses only a comma
, creating a comma splice.

That matches B.
